title: 'Street rights' domain (police encounters / recording / protest) — deferred, extra safety review
context: Police encounters, the right to record, and protest rights are important civil-rights topics for Know-Your-Rights, but they carry the highest physical-danger profile: an error or misunderstanding in this domain can get someone hurt, arrested, or detained. These topics are deferred to after M6, behind the hardened review apparatus proven on tenant/worker content, and require extra safety review — a licensed attorney plus a practitioner reviewer with relevant experience. The content must never advise escalation or resistance; it states what the law permits, requires, and prohibits in a named jurisdiction, neutrally and completely. This task is backlog: not started until M6 is complete.
objective: Draft and iterate to final form plain-language 'street rights' explainers for a confirmed jurisdiction covering police encounter rights, the right to record, and protest rights. Content is limited to what the law of the named jurisdiction says, stated neutrally. The content must never advise escalation, resistance to a lawful order, or any action that increases physical danger. Requires extra safety review: licensed-attorney sign-off plus a practitioner reviewer (civil-liberties attorney or experienced civil-rights advocate) before delivery.
acceptanceCriteria: Explainers produced covering police encounter rights, the right to record law enforcement in public, and protest and assembly rights for the confirmed jurisdiction — each as general legal information, not individualized advice

Content is strictly factual and neutral: states what the law of the named jurisdiction permits, requires, and prohibits; never advises escalation, resistance to a lawful order, or any action that increases physical danger to the reader
Every legal claim is primary-source-cited to the named jurisdiction (statute, constitutional provision, court ruling, official guidance); source reuse terms verified
All content passes the full content-lint suite (task 004) with special attention to neutrality lint and non-advice lint; extra red-team review for 'resist/evade' framing applied
Carries the 'not legal advice' banner and a prominent safety note ('In an encounter, safety first; comply with lawful orders; assert rights calmly and through legal channels') and route-to-help block
Licensed-attorney sign-off recorded (admitted in the jurisdiction, with civil-rights or criminal-defense experience) before delivery
Extra safety review recorded: a practitioner reviewer (civil-liberties attorney or experienced civil-rights advocate) has reviewed for physical-danger failure modes and approved
Sequenced after M6; not started until the review apparatus is proven on tenant/worker content
output: Plain-language 'street rights' explainers (police encounters, right to record, protest rights) for the confirmed jurisdiction — schema-valid, primary-source-cited, lint-clean, safety-reviewed, and signed off by both a licensed attorney and a practitioner reviewer — the highest-risk domain in the Know-Your-Rights commons.
